name: Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15
name_original: Ibón de Plan (Basa de la Mora) – trail running desde el Refugio de Lavasar
description: Jog this difficult 2.1-mile (3.4 km) trail to the stunning Ibón de Plan (Basa de la Mora) in Parque Natural Posets-Maladeta.
region_intro: Jogging around Plan offers routes through a mountainous landscape characterized by high-altitude lakes (ibones) and mountain passes (collados). The region features trails that ascend through varied terrain, often leading to scenic water bodies. Many routes traverse the Pyrenean foothills, providing a mix of challenging climbs and rewarding views. The area is known for its natural beauty, with paths winding through alpine environments. Best jogging routes around Plan The most popular jogging route is Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes 32 minutes to complete. This route leads to the scenic Ibón de Plan lake. Another top favourite among local runners is PR-HU 115 Trail to Ibón del Sen, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.6 km) path. This trail traverses varied terrain towards Ibón del Sen. Local runners also love the Ibones de Barbarisa Trail from Collado de Sahún, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through mountain passes and high-altitude lakes, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Jogging around Plan is defined by high-mountain lakes, challenging ascents, and varied alpine terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are difficult. The routes in Plan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Plan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Plan?
A: There are over 60 running routes around Plan, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. While many routes are challenging, there are also some moderate and easy paths to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Plan?
A: Jogging routes in Plan typically feature a mountainous landscape with varied alpine terrain. You'll encounter high-altitude lakes (ibones), mountain passes (collados), and paths winding through the Pyrenean foothills. Expect challenging ascents and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any easy running trails suitable for beginners in Plan?
A: While many routes in Plan are considered difficult, there are a few easier options available. Komoot lists 3 easy routes and 19 moderate routes, providing choices for those new to mountain running or looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Plan?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37720632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from San Juan de Plan&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 11.7 km circular path with significant elevation gain, perfect for an immersive experience.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions I can see while running in Plan?
A: Many running routes in Plan lead to stunning natural attractions. You can encounter beautiful waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4105529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espigantosa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/794060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Barranco de Liri&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its high-altitude lakes, such as Ibón de Plan and Ibón del Sen, which are often the focal points of popular trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Plan?
A: Yes, Plan offers several longer running trails for endurance enthusiasts. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1395718900/pr-hu-115-trail-to-ibon-del-sen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-HU 115 Trail to Ibón del Sen&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 16.6 km path traversing varied terrain. Another challenging long route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28861495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavasar Shelter – Ibón de Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from Los Vives&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 18 km.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Plan?
A: The running routes in Plan are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. Runners often praise the challenging ascents, the beauty of the high-mountain lakes, and the varied alpine terrain that defines the region.

Q: Are there any refuges or huts along the running routes in Plan?
A: Yes, you can find several refuges and huts in the vicinity of Plan's running routes, which can be useful for longer runs or multi-day adventures. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4971893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viadós Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/877756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ángel Orús Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5035565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavasar Refuge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is a good running route to experience the high-altitude lakes of Plan?
A: To experience the high-altitude lakes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e948284525/ibones-de-barbarisa-trail-from-collado-de-sahun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibones de Barbarisa Trail from Collado de Sahún&lt;/a&gt;. This 9.3 km route leads through mountain passes and offers stunning views of multiple ibones (lakes).

Q: Is Plan suitable for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. Plan is particularly well-suited for experienced trail runners, with 44 out of 66 routes classified as difficult. The region offers challenging climbs, significant elevation changes, and technical terrain that will test your limits and reward you with breathtaking alpine scenery.

Q: What makes the Ibón de Plan a popular destination for runners?
A: The Ibón de Plan is a highly popular destination due to its scenic beauty and accessibility. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e948177691/ibon-de-plan-trail-via-gr-15" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively shorter (3.4 km) but difficult route that leads directly to this stunning high-altitude lake, making it a rewarding run for those seeking iconic views.
tips:
The Plan or Basa de la Mora lake is located at an altitude of 1,910 meters and fills up after the thaw each winter.
The name of Basa de la Mora (or "Basa la Mora" in Aragonese) comes from a local folktale according to which a Moorish princess drowned in this lake while trying to escape Christian troops. According to this legend, during the night of San Juan, people who wash their faces with the lake water can see the princess dancing on the surface.

This unguarded refuge is very close to Ibón de Plan and provides a 360 degree panoramic view. On one side are the sharp ridges of the mountains that surround the glacial cirque, and on the other side you can see, in the distance, the Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15?
faqs.answer: The most common starting point for this route is from Saravillo, where you can find parking. Another access point is near the Lavasar Refuge, which is closer to the lake itself and offers a more comfortable final stretch.

faqs.question: Is the Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15 dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, as it passes through the Parque Natural Posets-Maladeta, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock, especially during grazing seasons. Always carry waste bags and ensure your dog has enough water.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to experience this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful throughout the warmer months. Late spring (May-June) offers lush meadows and blooming flowers, while summer (July-August) provides a fresh environment with ample shade from the pine forests. Autumn (September-October) brings stunning colors to the landscape. Always check weather conditions before heading out, as mountain weather can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15. However, it is located within the Parque Natural Posets-Maladeta, so visitors are expected to respect park regulations.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when jogging in Parque Natural Posets-Maladeta?
faqs.answer: When jogging within the Parque Natural Posets-Maladeta, it's important to stay on marked paths, avoid disturbing wildlife, and carry out all your trash. Camping is generally restricted, and fires are strictly prohibited. Respect local flora and fauna, and keep dogs on a leash. These rules help preserve the natural beauty and ecological importance of the area.

faqs.question: Is there a recommended starting point for a more manageable jogging experience to Ibón de Plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, for a more 'bearable' and less steep jogging experience, it's recommended to approach the Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) via the GR 15 route starting from Saravillo. This route offers a progressive incline, making it more manageable compared to the significantly steeper alternative from Plan village.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route to Ibón de Plan via GR 15 intersects with and follows sections of several other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Sendero Prepirenaico, the Ibón de Plan / Basa de La Mora trail, and various segments of the GR-15 itself.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of well-marked paths, often traversing through dense pine forests and open, flowered meadows. While generally easy to navigate, expect typical mountain trail conditions including stones, roots, and occasional fallen logs. Some sections can be steep, requiring more effort, but the final stretch to the lake is comfortable with minimal elevation change.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking panoramas, including views of the Chistau valley and the imposing limestone walls of the Cotiella massif. You'll pass through expansive forests providing ample shade. The destination, Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora), is a stunning glacial lake nestled amidst towering peaks. Near the Lavasar Refuge, you can also find remnants of megalithic stone circles known as 'cromlechs'.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for jogging this specific segment of the trail is approximately 32 minutes. However, the total time can vary significantly depending on your starting point (e.g., Saravillo vs. Lavasar Refuge), your fitness level, and how much time you spend enjoying the scenery at the Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora).

faqs.question: Is the Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15 a loop trail?
faqs.answer: This specific route segment is primarily an out-and-back trail to the Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora). While it connects to a larger network of trails, including the GR 15, the most direct approach to the lake is not a closed loop.

waypoints.name: Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora)
